steps.title: Identify the passage's central claim
steps.content: The passage opens with "We are creatures of the sun. We love light and life. That is why we crowd into the towns and cities." The author's purpose is to explain why people congregate in cities — it stems from shared traits (love of light and life), not sadness, a preference for rural settings, or any caution about dismissing nature.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To convey that crowded areas can cause people to experience feelings of sadness': The passage links crowding to love of light and life, not sadness. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To caution people not to be so quick to dismiss the natural beauty that can be found in rural areas': The passage makes no argument for rural areas or any caution about dismissing them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To illustrate the idea that most people tend to prefer hillsides in the country to certain aspects of towns and cities': This reverses the passage's stated preference — the author says people choose cities, not country hillsides. ✗
  • Choice D — 'To address common traits that motivate many people to choose to live in urban environments': Directly matches the passage's explanation that shared traits — craving light and life — drive people to cities. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
